steps.content: Find the greatest common factor (GCF) of both terms. $10x^2$ has factors $1, 2, 5, 10$; $130$ has factors $1, 2, 5, 10, 13, \ldots$ The GCF is $10$. Factor it out: $$10x^2 + 130 = 10(x^2 + 13)$$

Evaluate all choices:
  • A: $10\left(13x^2\right)$ — Distributing gives $130x^2$, which is not the original expression. Incorrect.
  • B: $10\left(x^2 + 13\right)$ — Distributing gives $10x^2 + 130$, which matches exactly. That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
  • C: $130\left(10x^2\right)$ — Distributing gives $1300x^2$, completely different. Incorrect.
  • D: $130\left(10x^2 + 1\right)$ — Distributing gives $1300x^2 + 130$, which adds a wrong coefficient on $x^2$. Incorrect.
